summary: Draft/WIP aggregate sweep PR with no loaded diff or changed-file metadata. The PR body says it was not ready for maintainer review or merge, still needed human QA, and should be broken into smaller PRs. A bot report showed broad matching gains across many units, but the PR was closed after the work was split into other PRs.

key_files.role: loaded diff; empty in slice, so no concrete changed files can be extracted
PR body and issue-comment evidence for WIP status, revert/pre-commit guidance, and closure reason
bot report and text-record source for matching summary and broad unit list

what_changed: No changed files are available in the loaded slice: changed_files is 0 and the raw diff is empty.
The PR body describes the branch as a draft/WIP sweep that was not ready for maintainer review or merge.
The bot report recorded GALE01 matching gains from f1fd53e to bc5d628: matched code 75.65% (+1.18%, +46000 bytes) and matched data 42.36% (+0.47%, +5728 bytes).
The bot report lists 103 new matches and 470 improvements in unmatched items, spanning menu, fighter common, THP, stage, interface, game, library, baselib, and toy/ty units.
The PR was closed by the author with the explanation that all broken-down PRs were now open.

smart_moves.text: The author explicitly marked the PR as draft/WIP and not ready for maintainer review or merge, preventing the aggregate sweep from being treated as review-ready.
evidence_refs: PR body: 'Draft/WIP: not ready for maintainer review or merge.'

smart_moves.text: The author encouraged cherry-picking useful pieces while the larger PR was being reorganized.
evidence_refs: PR body: 'If anything in this PR helps move your work forward, please cherry pick it out, I can rebase as needed.'

smart_moves.text: The author closed the aggregate PR after the work was split into smaller PRs.
evidence_refs: issuecomment-4734467843: 'Closing this PR as all broken down PRs are now open'

decomp_lessons.lesson: A very broad matching sweep can be useful as a staging or discovery branch, but the loaded evidence supports treating it as intake material to split and QA rather than as a reviewable final decomp PR.
evidence_refs: PR body: 'Still need to do human QA and break down into more manageable PRs'

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use the automated GALE01 report as a coarse signal for broad matching gains, but do not infer file-level source tactics from it when no diff is loaded.
evidence_refs: text_corpus decomp-dev[bot] report: matched code/data percentages, 103 new matches, 470 improvements

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For the tooling-formatting commit identified in discussion, reproduction was described as checking out upstream/master versions of tools, .github, .pre-commit-config.yaml, and .clang-format, then running pre-commit over all files.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Suggested that commit e3759d3a8893bea1e2dd6bd822c7bae467d9d748 could be reverted if not useful, and gave reproduction commands using upstream/master checkout of tooling/config files plus pre-commit.

follow_up_queries: Find the broken-down PRs opened after PR 2702 closure
Search PRs mentioning 'Big Sweep Round 2' or branch 'codex/split-up/mn'
Search commit e3759d3a8893bea1e2dd6bd822c7bae467d9d748 for tooling/pre-commit changes
Search later PRs for THPDec matches from this sweep
Search later PRs for mnmainrule data and mn_80231804
Search later PRs for ftCommon ftCo_0A01 matches

notes: No diff content or changed-file metadata is available in the slice, so file-specific changes, exact source edits, naming changes, and matching tactics cannot be verified.
The bot report provides quantitative matching outcomes and affected unit names, but it does not show the implementation details behind those matches.
There were no review comments and no formal review records in the loaded metadata.
The PR was closed with merged_at null; the slice supports closure after breakdown into other PRs, not merge or acceptance of this aggregate branch.

source_update_candidates.title: Large WIP decomp sweeps should be split before review
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2702 is evidence that a broad decomp sweep with many matching gains was intentionally kept draft/WIP, marked as needing human QA, offered for cherry-picking, and later closed once broken-down PRs were open. Curator may consider adding this as process guidance, not as a code-level decomp standard.
evidence_refs: PR body: 'Draft/WIP: not ready for maintainer review or merge.'
PR body: 'Still need to do human QA and break down into more manageable PRs'
PR body: 'please cherry pick it out'
source_update_candidates.reason: The evidence supports process guidance for aggregate WIP branches, but not a source-code standard or path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not create file/path facts for the units listed in the bot report from this slice alone; no diff or source excerpt shows what changed.
Do not promote the reported matches as accepted implementation lessons; the PR was WIP, unmerged, and explicitly required human QA and breakdown.
Do not infer naming conventions from the bot report item names; they are unit/symbol labels, not evidence of author naming decisions in the diff.
